description_long: This is an acoustic guitar with a classic dreadnought body shape, featuring a light wood finish with visible grain patterns and some areas of discoloration or wear. The soundhole is centrally located on the top face, surrounded by a subtle rosette. The neck is long and slender, leading to a dark-colored headstock with six tuning pegs arranged symmetrically, three on each side. The fretboard is marked with visible frets and appears to be made of a darker wood or composite material. The bridge and saddle are positioned below the soundhole, anchoring the strings to the body. The overall appearance suggests a well-used instrument, possibly exposed to outdoor or urban environments.
description: A well-worn acoustic guitar with a light wood body, dark neck, and six tuning pegs, showing signs of use and discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the guitar's soundhole, bridge, and the full length of the neck and headstock.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the guitar's body thickness, neck profile, and the bridge's elevation above the body.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the body and the neck, with the headstock and tuning pegs visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the guitar's body curve, neck, and the transition between the body and neck.
attribute_headstock_design: Standard acoustic headstock with six tuning pegs, three per side, no visible logo.
